hcf0f18b9g9f02g4fe7g9dabg1716830db367: Are you now? Don't make promises you can't keep, darling.
hc928b0dbg66cag476fgb242g237ec0e03f88: Thankfully I've had my needs met this evening. I found a bear.
h0a4f7d3dg9cbcg4700ga4cag45cbad0e743a: He took a little of my blood, I took all of his.
hd433971eg0752g4832g93f8g21717ba7dbf5: It's nothing compared to - well, other things I could be dining on. But significantly better than the rats and bugs Cazador served me.
h00896ffeg72e2g4f61ga13ega554ca1b460f: You seem happy. No need to drink humanoid blood, then?
h9d3fefafge5bfg41e3g9740g7ee6b39a64f6: You're comparing plonk to vintage wine. You can make merry with either, but they are not the same.
h7754de88gbfa0g404agacceg7a10b03e25e0: But Cazador fed me rats and bugs. And when you're used to drinking from a sewer, even plonk is a marked improvement.
h9463169eg6b67g4d11gbadfg0f66b3968992: As long as you're leaving mine alone.
h4d91e752gdc2cg481ag9679g3fb5d5f069c4: You think I'd kill you, just like that? Darling, I would never!
h25975047g542fg4475g81b8gea31c846708f: Anyway, the bear will sustain me. And it's a marked improvement over the rats and flies Cazador would feed me.
hb98f49b8gd99eg4252g8f76gc4e15f08aeda: It was exactly as appetising as you'd think.
h34765bf8gb294g4b0eg827dg96a848d729d9: Still, that was the past. I'll never have to grovel for him again.
h0db7fef5g9de1g41f5gafbcg1721d77980c2: True, you can start over. You can be better than what he made you.
hca5b1159ga11fg4d0fg8020ge609b3184391: Exactly! I can be better than him. Stronger. More powerful. More -
hdb43c6b5gd5a3g46e4gbfbagff3d92e8505f: - oh, you meant 'be kinder'? Pet bunnies, that sort of thing?
hec24259bg3d46g4b6fgaea8gd8c9a4920ebe: I've no objection to being nice, of course. Once I have the power to bend others to my will.
hb6026792g5c21g4874g9d32g29f0fcb0e646: Maybe not, but you'll grovel before the mind flayers if you keep using the tadpole.
h330e1046g3470g4486g9fadg4c13e4634725: I don't intend to grovel before anyone!
h3a2c0820gb9bbg4828gb181gbd59090a6376: The mind flayers gave us power, but left us to our own devices. Whatever they had planned for us, they failed.
h58378f8fg83f5g4994g9088g0245520dd916: And now I'm left with this gift: the strength to take my own freedom. I'm not afraid to use it, and you shouldn't be either.
hc9e13144g9628g42fdgbc8bgc5cad2c83989: Well - look at us. We've never been more powerful.
ha5d3be97g6729g4c9bga5a2g73e48c9d78ab: And being able to walk in the sunlight opens a lot of doors for me. Maybe some coffins too.
h4163f8b1gfafbg4cb9g8149g2cdd109bbb73: No, these tadpoles are a gift I intend to use to the fullest. You should do the same.
hde36f5dbge9ecg4295g9beeg5e56700389e3: Does it matter? Blood is blood, isn't it?
hfe20dd3ag6e2ag49c7g97ccgb8abd9fd7c8f: Not at all! Blood is life essence. And some creatures have so much more life than others.
h891587a3gad55g4605g8f5egeb498d2b4a82: Cazador gave me just enough to keep me - well, 'alive', for lack of a better word. But never more than that.
h6015a67dg350bg4e28gb4c7gc2227c845554: Flies? And what did you do to deserve that?
h583697edg1959g4fb8gb59dg58fdd3e04468: I existed, that was enough for him. He revelled in having power over me.
h90db8a94ge1bdg4e40gb047gc3386ead0fff: Because those with power can do whatever the hell they want.
h6ff83861ge618g4f6cga425g6cdcd0715f84: You think power lets you do anything, free from consequences?
h8c2694e3gf5d9g4a99gbb8fg807fa43d1eb0: You can't look at the world and tell me I'm wrong.
hcd6c073fg3812g40e5g9fd3ge63003a039a2: The strong have a duty to protect the weak.
h0d7c810ag401dg4ed6gb94eg0633185bfe14: They're doing a piss poor job, then.
h4e023784gefcdg4c81gabfcg18512b10a428: The strong had two centuries to pluck me from torture, but no one came. No, it was the mind flayers that rescued me.
h53559217g0907g4d34gbbdegcf98f04cb711: They gave me a gift: the strength to take my own freedom. I'm embracing this power - you should too.
hfc78302bgb109g48d2ga616ge8e5bf8420c8: Power corrupts. You'd do well to remember that.
h415945c6gff2dg4b67g92efgf7801f407d96: Oh I hope so. A little corruption sounds fun.
h6d2806c3g8787g4feag8037gf5c6f59ed3f7: I spent centuries as the victim of a corrupt man. It was the mind flayers that plucked me away from that.
h5fd8367dgffe6g4f4bg8e62g37764226e7cb: Careful, there are heroes about. I'd hate for them to get the right idea about you.
h0829d2a1g6454g4044g9b6dg86ec3b26888b: Let them. Heroes are half the damn problem. Fools that believe in right and wrong - good and evil.
h93f21d95gc431g4e69g9897g8683ee4cfd67: Heroes didn't save me from Cazador: mind flayers did.
ha2ebc52dg0befg4336gba20ga81781c14403: You're free now. That's what matters.
h6522a945g0864g413dg887dgea164467d863: Is it? What good is freedom if I'm always watching the shadows?
h81693962ga54bg489cgb146g6c0b90515aad: No, I'll be safe when I'm powerful enough to grind Cazador into the dust. Powerful enough to do whatever the hell I want.
he2be4b8ege419g4a49ga09egab9dfa176ec0: We're not powerless. The tadpoles are quite the asset.
h6af796a7g39d0g4186gba88gd0608e3d21a6: Indeed they are. Now that I can walk in the sun - well, that opens all manner of doors.
h56a249eagdc5cg414bg8adeg32347be3766c: It could open some coffins too.
hd766ea54gb9dfg4023g9c2egff3777cff0e8: Oh, that. A scratch. I ran afoul of a bear.
hb7ab7707gd036g4c1egba14ge379fd98e4ef: I have drunk. Not alcohol, of course. A bear.
